apple_black_rot.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies an apple leaf with symptoms compatible with black rot, so the tree should not be considered healthy for this image.
apple_black_rot.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by infected leaves or fruits left in the orchard and by high humidity
visual_symptoms: brown or black spots on leaves
dry areas on branches or mummified fruit
soil_recommendation: this is not primarily a soil problem
keep fertilization balanced and avoid excess nitrogen
add mature compost only as general support for tree vigor
test the soil before adding large amounts of potassium, calcium or pH amendments
treatment: remove heavily infected leaves, fruit and branches
do not leave mummified fruit on the tree or on the ground
prune the canopy to improve air circulation
use a preventive fungicide according to the product label and local regulations

apple_cedar_apple_rust.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies an apple leaf with rust-like symptoms, so the diagnosis is treated as disease rather than a healthy leaf.
apple_cedar_apple_rust.likely_cause: fungal infection that alternates between apple and juniper or ornamental cedar hosts
visual_symptoms: orange or yellow spots on leaves
dark points in the center of the spots
premature leaf drop in severe cases
soil_recommendation: this is not solved by adding a single nutrient to the soil
keep the soil well drained and cover it with organic mulch
avoid excess nitrogen because it can produce tender growth that is more sensitive
treatment: check whether infected junipers are present nearby
remove heavily affected leaves when practical
apply preventive fungicide during risk periods before strong infections occur

apple_healthy.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies an apple leaf without visible symptoms from the known classes, so the leaf is considered healthy.
apple_healthy.likely_cause: no clear symptoms were detected in the known classes
visual_symptoms: no large spots, rust or visible mold
soil_recommendation: do not add treatments without a reason
maintain organic mulch and mature compost in moderate amounts
periodically check soil pH and moisture

apple_scab.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies an apple leaf with symptoms compatible with apple scab, so the tree requires monitoring and preventive treatment.
apple_scab.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by wet leaves and infected plant debris
visual_symptoms: rough spots on fruit in advanced cases
soil_recommendation: this is not mainly a nutrient deficiency
collect fallen leaves because they can preserve spores
add mature compost and mulch for general soil health
avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization
treatment: remove fallen leaves and infected material
apply preventive fungicide during wet periods

strawberry_healthy.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a strawberry leaf without evident symptoms from the known classes, so the leaf is considered healthy.
strawberry_healthy.likely_cause: no clear symptoms were detected in the known classes
soil_recommendation: do not add treatments without symptoms
keep the soil well drained and rich in organic matter
use mulch to stabilize moisture and reduce leaf contact with soil

strawberry_leaf_scorch.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a strawberry leaf with scorch or spotting symptoms, so the image is treated as diseased.
strawberry_leaf_scorch.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by humidity, dense planting and infected plant debris
visual_symptoms: brown or purple spots on leaves
necrotic areas that may merge in severe attacks
soil_recommendation: this is not mainly a nutrient problem
maintain good drainage and avoid excess nitrogen
remove infected plant debris
treatment: increase spacing and air circulation between plants
apply an approved fungicide if the attack expands

tomato_healthy.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf without visible symptoms from the known classes, so the leaf is considered healthy.
tomato_healthy.likely_cause: no clear symptoms were detected in the known classes
soil_recommendation: do not add treatments without symptoms
maintain balanced fertilization and good drainage
add mature compost in moderate amounts
treatment: ensure good air circulation between plants

tomato_bacterial_spot.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with small dark spots compatible with bacterial spot.
tomato_bacterial_spot.likely_cause: bacterial infection favored by water on leaves and splash dispersal
soil_recommendation: this is not corrected by a simple nutrient addition
avoid excess nitrogen
keep mulch in place to reduce soil splash onto leaves
treatment: apply approved products for bacterial diseases according to the label

tomato_early_blight.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with concentric spots compatible with early blight.
tomato_early_blight.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by humidity and infected plant debris
soil_recommendation: remove infected plant debris
keep mulch in place to reduce splash dispersal
avoid excess nitrogen
treatment: apply approved preventive fungicide during wet periods

tomato_late_blight.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with extended lesions and a water-soaked appearance compatible with late blight.
tomato_late_blight.likely_cause: oomycete infection favored by cool, wet weather and wet leaves
visual_symptoms: possible whitish growth on the underside in wet conditions
soil_recommendation: this is not a soil deficiency
avoid wetting the leaves
quickly remove heavily affected plants or leaves
treatment: isolate or remove heavily infected material
apply approved preventive treatments during high-risk periods

tomato_leaf_mold.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with symptoms of leaf mold.
tomato_leaf_mold.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by high humidity and poor ventilation
visual_symptoms: yellow spots on the upper leaf surface
soil_recommendation: this is not solved directly through fertilization
reduce humidity and improve ventilation
avoid watering leaves
treatment: apply an approved fungicide if the disease progresses

tomato_septoria_leaf_spot.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with many small spots compatible with Septoria.
tomato_septoria_leaf_spot.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by wet leaves and infected plant debris
visual_symptoms: small round spots with light centers
soil_recommendation: collect infected plant debris
use mulch to reduce splash dispersal
maintain balanced fertilization
treatment: apply preventive fungicide during wet periods

tomato_spider_mites.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with symptoms compatible with spider mites.
tomato_spider_mites.likely_cause: mite attack favored by hot and dry weather
soil_recommendation: this is not a soil problem
avoid water stress
keep plants vigorous through balanced watering
treatment: use biological control or approved miticides if the attack is strong

tomato_target_spot.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with circular target-like spots.
tomato_target_spot.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by humidity and poor air circulation
soil_recommendation: this is not solved by a quick soil addition
maintain mulch and good drainage
avoid excess nitrogen
treatment: apply an approved fungicide during risk periods

tomato_mosaic_virus.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with mosaic-like discoloration compatible with tomato mosaic virus.
tomato_mosaic_virus.likely_cause: viral infection transmitted through infected plant material, mechanical contact or contaminated tools
soil_recommendation: there is no soil correction for this virus
do not over-fertilize in an attempt to mask symptoms
maintain crop hygiene
treatment: use certified seedlings and avoid handling wet plants

tomato_yellow_leaf_curl_virus.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a tomato leaf with yellowing and curling compatible with yellow leaf curl virus.
tomato_yellow_leaf_curl_virus.likely_cause: viral infection often transmitted by whiteflies
soil_recommendation: this is not treated through soil additions
control host weeds and insect vectors
keep plants free from water stress
treatment: use healthy seedlings and insect netting where possible

cherry_healthy.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a cherry leaf without visible symptoms from the known classes, so the leaf is considered healthy.
cherry_healthy.likely_cause: no clear symptoms were detected in the known classes
soil_recommendation: do not add treatments without symptoms
maintain good drainage
use mature compost in moderate amounts

cherry_powdery_mildew.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a cherry leaf with whitish powdery deposits, so the diagnosis is powdery mildew.
cherry_powdery_mildew.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by tender growth, humidity and poor ventilation
soil_recommendation: avoid excess nitrogen
maintain good drainage and organic mulch
mature compost can support vigor but does not directly treat powdery mildew
treatment: use approved powdery mildew treatments such as sulfur-based products or locally recommended products

cherry_leaf_spot.symbolic_summary: The symbolic rule identifies a cherry leaf with circular spots and necrotic areas, so the image is treated as leaf spot disease.
cherry_leaf_spot.likely_cause: fungal infection favored by high humidity, infected fallen leaves and poor canopy ventilation
visual_symptoms: small brown or purple spots on leaves
premature leaf drop in severe attacks
soil_recommendation: this is not corrected directly by a simple soil addition
collect and remove infected fallen leaves
maintain good drainage and avoid excess nitrogen
treatment: remove affected leaves and plant debris
apply approved preventive treatments for leaf spot during risk periods
